prompt: Square 1024x1024 surreal masterpiece rendering Simpson’s Paradox essence via water: three distinct, separated streams of water from identical springs, each stream visibly flowing downward in parallel channels with consistent speed and volume, captured mid-flow. Where the three channels converge into one wide river, the water surface paradoxically rises upward in a smooth, impossible ascent against gravity, as if the merger creates upward momentum. Render hyper-realistically with refractive light, foam details, subtle channel distinctions via stone textures and moss, so the viewer perceives the subgroups descending but the whole ascending as an uncanny physical law. No text, numbers, charts, math, symbols. Pure perceptual contradiction feels inevitable in the fluid dynamics, visually unique eternal cascade composition.
visual_logic: Separate streams descend; merged river ascends, embodying subgroup trends reversing in aggregate as perceptual fluid impossibility.
